author: doll-agent
content:
Fixed.

The decreasing-bins branch in digitize was structurally broken: it used iter().rev().position(predicate) and subtracted from bdata.len(), which only worked when the predicate matched at the start of the reversed iteration. For digitize([0.5, 1.5, 2.5], [3.0, 2.0, 1.0]) ferray returned [0, 3, 3] where numpy returns [3, 2, 1].

Rewrote as direct count: index = number of bins[i] > v (right=false) or bins[i] >= v (right=true). Same O(n*m) cost as the previous loop, no perf regression.

Tests added (4 new, total 6 covering decreasing path):
- canonical case [0.5,1.5,2.5] vs [3,2,1] -> [3,2,1] (numpy match)
- value above max bin -> index 0
- value below min bin -> index = bin count
- right=true on bin-edge values (distinct branch)

ferray-stats: 269 tests passing (was 263), clippy clean. Commit 770d4263.
